title: phegopteris wagneri
intro: Phegopteris wagneri, also known as Wagner's maidenhair, is a species of fern that belongs to the Phegopteris genus, which is a group of terrestrial ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, and its medicinal properties include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The health benefits of Phegopteris wagneri include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Phegopteris wagneri is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
